# LedeWire JS SDK

> Official JavaScript/TypeScript SDK for the LedeWire API.
> Source: https://github.com/ledewire/ledewire-js-sdk
> Docs: https://ledewire.github.io/ledewire-js-sdk/
> API Reference: https://ledewire.github.io/ledewire-js-sdk/api/

## Packages

- `@ledewire/browser` — Browser SDK (ESM + CDN IIFE `Ledewire` global). Buyer flows only.
- `@ledewire/node` — Node.js SDK. Full API: merchant, seller, and buyer flows.

## Browser CDN usage

```html
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@ledewire/browser@1/dist/ledewire.min.js"></script>
<script>
  const lw = Ledewire.init({ apiKey: 'your_api_key' })
  const state = await lw.checkout.state('content-id')
  // state.checkout_state.next_required_action:
  //   'authenticate' | 'fund_wallet' | 'purchase' | 'view_content'
</script>
```

## Node.js usage

```ts
import { createClient } from '@ledewire/node'
const client = createClient({
  apiKey: process.env.LEDEWIRE_API_KEY,
  apiSecret: process.env.LEDEWIRE_API_SECRET,
})
```

## Client namespaces

### @ledewire/browser — init()
- `lw.auth.signup(body)` — create buyer account
- `lw.auth.loginWithEmail({ email, password })` — buyer email login
- `lw.auth.loginWithGoogle({ id_token })` — buyer Google login
- `lw.checkout.state(contentId)` → `CheckoutStateResponse` — next action for content
- `lw.wallet.balance()` → `{ balance_cents }` — buyer wallet balance
- `lw.wallet.transactions()` → list of wallet transactions
- `lw.wallet.createPaymentSession({ amount_cents })` → Stripe payment session
- `lw.wallet.getPaymentStatus(sessionId)` → payment status
- `lw.purchases.create({ content_id })` → create purchase
- `lw.purchases.list()` → list purchases
- `lw.purchases.get(id)` → single purchase
- `lw.content.getWithAccess(contentId, options?)` → content + access info

### @ledewire/node — createClient()
- `client.auth.*` — buyer auth (signup, email, google, logout, password reset)
- `client.merchant.auth.*` — merchant email/google login; `.stores()` → store list
- `client.merchant.users.*` — team management (list, invite, remove)
- `client.merchant.sales.*` — summary, list, get; `buyers(storeId)`
- `client.merchant.config.get(storeId)` → store config (google_client_id)
- `client.seller.content.*` — content CRUD + search
- `client.wallet.*` — balance, transactions, createPaymentSession, getPaymentStatus
- `client.purchases.*` — create, list, get
- `client.content.getWithAccess(contentId, options?)` → content + access info
- `client.checkout.state(contentId)` → `CheckoutStateResponse`

## Key types (packages/core)

```ts
interface CheckoutStateResponse {
  content_id: string
  content_title: string
  price_cents: number
  checkout_state: {
    is_authenticated: boolean
    has_sufficient_funds: boolean | null
    has_purchased: boolean
    next_required_action: 'authenticate' | 'fund_wallet' | 'purchase' | 'view_content'
  }
}

// All SDK errors extend LedewireError
class LedewireError extends Error { statusCode: number; code: number }
class UnauthorizedError extends LedewireError {}
class NotFoundError extends LedewireError {}
class ValidationError extends LedewireError {}
class ServerError extends LedewireError {}
```

## Architecture notes

- `packages/core` is private (not published). Shared by browser and node packages.
- `TokenManager` handles JWT refresh automatically — do NOT manually refresh.
- HTTP errors are mapped to typed error classes in `packages/core/src/errors.ts`.
- OpenAPI source of truth: `ledewire.yml` at the repo root.
- All exported public symbols have JSDoc (enforced by ESLint `jsdoc/require-jsdoc`).
- Tests use Vitest + MSW for HTTP mocking. Coverage ≥ 90% enforced in CI.
